top_stocks.description:
• Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) is a diversified semiconductor and infrastructure software company, with major operations in custom silicon/accelerators, networking/AI interconnect, and enterprise infrastructure software.
• Broadcom is widely regarded as a large-cap, mega-cap technology provider (typically tracked as a member of the largest US equity market groupings by market capitalization).
• Competitive advantage: broad AI infrastructure exposure (custom silicon + networking) and strong scale/operating leverage in both chips and infrastructure software, supporting bundled solutions for large customers.
top_stocks.latest_news:
• 2026-03-04: Broadcom reported first quarter fiscal 2026 results, including Q1 AI revenue of $8.4B (up 106% year-over-year) and guided second-quarter fiscal 2026 revenue of approximately $22.0B (up 47% from the prior year quarter). 
• 2026-03-04: Broadcom also announced a quarterly dividend alongside the fiscal Q1 results.
• 2026-03 (per company filings): Broadcom’s board authorized a new stock repurchase program to repurchase up to $10B of common stock through 2026-12-31.
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E=81.58 with PEG=0.48 — the P/E looks demanding, but the low PEG suggests the market is pricing in growth faster than the P/E would normally imply; whether it’s “reasonable” depends on sustaining AI-driven revenue expansion and margins.
• Profitability: ROE=0.3337% and profit margin=0.3657% — on the face of the screener inputs these margins are modest (especially ROE), so margin/return durability should be validated against the business mix and accounting effects.
• Growth: EPS next 5Y=0.4864% — the screener’s long-run EPS growth rate is low; for an 81x P/E to be justified, investors typically expect stronger earnings trajectory than this single-digit-from-now estimate.
• Quality: Investor Score=100/100 — this indicates strong screen-based quality/consistency; combining that with AI infrastructure momentum can help explain the premium multiple, but the main risk is paying up if growth normalizes.

top_stocks.description:
• Micron Technology (MU) designs and manufactures memory and storage products (DRAM, NAND, and related memory solutions) used across PCs, mobile devices, datacenter/AI systems, and embedded applications.
• Micron is a large-cap semiconductor manufacturer and a major supplier in the global memory cycle.
• Competitive advantage: leadership position in memory supply and products used in AI data centers (including DRAM/NAND demand tied to AI training/inference infrastructure), benefiting from industry supply discipline and technology transitions.
top_stocks.latest_news:
• 2026-02 (company reporting): Micron announced results for fiscal Q1 2026, stating it delivered record revenue and significant margin expansion and provided an outlook for fiscal Q2.
• 2026-03-18: Micron scheduled to report fiscal second-quarter results on this date (published as an investor communication ahead of the earnings release).
• 2026-01-14: Micron’s quarterly dividend payable date (dividend declared for the period, with payment on 2026-01-14 as noted in its Q1 fiscal 2026 materials).
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E=24.77 with PEG=0.05 — the PEG is extremely low relative to the P/E, which can indicate very favorable implied growth (or potentially a scoring artifact / near-term normalization dynamics); it warrants checking that growth expectations are sustained.
• Profitability: ROE=0.3982% and profit margin=0.4149% — these input values suggest relatively modest returns; however, memory cycles can cause sharp swings, so profitability should be assessed alongside cycle position.
• Growth: EPS next 5Y=1.1519% — modest long-run growth per screener inputs, though “This Y” EPS growth is much higher (5.8731%), implying near-term upcycle strength; MU is most attractive when the memory upcycle continues.
• Quality: Investor Score=100/100 — screen suggests strong overall fundamental profile; the primary debate is whether MU’s earnings power can remain elevated beyond the current cycle.

top_stocks.description:
• NVIDIA (NVDA) designs GPUs and accelerated computing platforms used for AI training and inference, and also provides networking, software platforms, and enterprise AI solutions.
• NVIDIA is a mega-cap technology leader and key “platform” supplier in AI compute.
• Competitive advantage: an integrated AI compute ecosystem (hardware + high-performance interconnect + software platform), strong developer adoption, and dominant positioning in large-scale AI datacenter deployments.
top_stocks.latest_news:
• 2025-05-28 (most recent official quarterly release found in search): NVIDIA announced financial results for first quarter fiscal 2026, reporting revenue of $44.1B (up 12% sequentially and 69% year-over-year) and GAAP diluted EPS of $0.76.
• 2026-04 (earnings activity context): multiple sources indicate NVIDIA’s April 2026 earnings report timing windows were active in early-to-mid April (useful for near-term event risk planning on 2026-04-27).
• 2026-02 (earnings context): NVIDIA’s fiscal 2026 fourth-quarter results and fiscal 2027 first-quarter revenue guidance were covered as part of the company’s earnings cycle reporting in February 2026.
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E=44.19 with PEG=0.49 — valuation is high but PEG < 1 suggests the market expects growth to justify a premium.
• Profitability: ROE=1.0149%, ROIC=71.75%, and profit margin=0.556% — ROIC is extremely strong per the screener input, aligning with NVIDIA’s perception of high economic returns during AI-led demand; ROE/margins should still be sanity-checked because inputs may be scaled or affected by accounting choices.
• Growth: EPS next 5Y=0.3938% — the screener’s long-run EPS growth estimate is low versus what investors often assume for NVDA; if long-run growth is capped, a 44x multiple becomes more vulnerable.
• Quality: Investor Score=100/100 — indicates top-tier screen signals; for NVDA, quality plus embedded platform advantages can support a premium, but event/guidance execution risk remains central.

top_stocks.description:
• Affiliated Managers Group (AMG) operates an affiliate-based investment management business, partnering with independent asset managers and earning fees tied to affiliate performance.
• AMG is typically classified as a mid-to-large-cap financial services company within asset management.
• Competitive advantage: its partnership model and ability to source/scale a diversified set of affiliate managers, including alternatives, creating an earnings stream with both structural and performance components.
top_stocks.latest_news:
• 2026-02-12: AMG announced it would report fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 results on this date (company investor relations release).
• 2026-02-12: AMG reported financial and operating results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2025 and also announced additional partnerships (including mentions of affiliates/partnership expansion in private markets and liquid alternatives). 
• 2026-04-23 (ahead of next cycle): AMG posted communications related to scheduling its first-quarter results conference call (timing/agenda update ahead of the next earnings event).
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E=12.73 with PEG=0.38 — valuation looks comparatively reasonable versus many growth-oriented sectors; PEG < 1 suggests the market prices growth more efficiently than the P/E alone.
• Profitability: ROE=0.2177%, ROIC=12.04%, profit margin=0.308% — ROIC is mid-range per the screen, while ROE/profit margin inputs look low; AMG’s economics can vary with AUM performance and incentive fee structures.
• Growth: EPS next 5Y=0.2029% — long-run growth per the screener is modest, so the case likely relies on capital return, affiliate expansion, and continued alternatives mix rather than aggressive EPS compounding.
• Quality: Investor Score=95/100 — indicates strong screen-based fundamentals; for AMG the key is whether earnings stability and alternatives AUM continue to offset market cycle volatility.

top_stocks.description:
• Centerra Gold (CGAU) is a gold mining company with a portfolio of mining operations and development assets, producing gold (and in some cases other by-products such as copper/zinc depending on operations).
• CGAU is a mid-cap mining equity, typically more sensitive to commodity price moves and operational execution.
• Competitive advantage: operating assets with cost discipline and a self-funded growth strategy focus (as described by the company), aiming to sustain production and reinvestment even amid commodity cyclicality.
top_stocks.latest_news:
• 2026-02-19: Centerra Gold reported fourth quarter and full year 2025 results, including robust production and beat cost guidance, and reiterated 2026 outlook alongside its self-funded growth strategy.
• 2026-04-29 (scheduled/expected): Centerra Gold Q1 2026 results were widely reported as due before market open/after market close around this date (earnings release timing communicated via earnings calendars/market sources).
• 2026-04-2026 (ongoing): Centerra Gold’s recent earnings communications highlighted operational and financial updates tied to mine performance and cost execution (context leading into the Q1 2026 reporting window).
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E=6.62 with PEG=0.42 — valuation is low, consistent with mining equities’ commodity sensitivity and/or expected volatility in earnings; PEG < 1 suggests the market expects growth that is not excessive for the multiple.
• Profitability: ROE=0.3143% and profit margin=0.4469% with ROIC=28.20% — ROIC is high per the screener input, which supports an argument for strong project-level economics, while ROE/margin appear low and may reflect capital structure/accounting and cycle effects.
• Growth: EPS next 5Y=0.2138% and Next Y=0.0606% — growth expectations are modest; at a low multiple, that can still be attractive if downside is limited by costs and production continuity.
• Quality: Investor Score=95/100 — screen indicates solid overall financial characteristics relative to peers; for CGAU the biggest swing factors are gold prices, operating costs, and any disruptions to production ramp or guidance.
